rank_note: Supports HAAD local process rules for coder references, query workflows, documentation, training, and claims accuracy.

rules: Coders must know the available references.
Coders must have access to HAAD Coding Manual, HAAD Adjudication rules, and other standards references.
Coders and staff must understand HAAD Code of Ethics.
Coder-physician query process must exist with timelines.
Pre-authorization workflows must have access to the patient's medical record.
Coding quality checks and resubmissions must be governed by policy.
Documentation must be completed within 24 hours.
Medical record must be complete.
Documentation must support medical necessity.
Reason for encounter, history, physical exam findings, and diagnostic results must be documented.
Assessment/clinical impression/diagnosis and plan of care must be documented.
Corrections, delayed entries, amendments, authentications, time, date, and signatures must be governed by policy.
PHI security/privacy must be protected.
EHR integrity must be maintained.
Copy-paste misuse must be controlled.
Narrative diagnosis is preferred over dropdown-only selection.
New staff must be oriented to the organization and technical applications.
Staff must receive ongoing training and education.
Staff must know their roles.
Training must fit the setting.
Coding staff should review HAAD guidelines, specialty topics, E/M documentation, ICD-10-CM awareness, and updates.

risk_areas: Violation of HAAD official coding guidelines
Documentation inadequate for billed level of service
Incorrect place or category of service
